coachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime, physical survival and magical survival: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 25. Take Krix: +18% on your effective HP at minute 15.

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: Bruiser Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for magical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 4,872 effective HP vs magical in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Bruiser Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 1,000 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +28% more one-combo burst: try Viper over Overlord (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Bruiser Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 7,304 effective HP vs physical in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Grux deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 33% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Steel deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 29% of its bite; you give up 33% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Bayle deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 33% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Aurora deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 33% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Zarus's main threat is Coliseum; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 33% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Crunch deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 33% of your damage.
